projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The sustained high device count (14–17 connected devices) over the last two hours, following an earlier jump from 11 to 16, strongly indicates an ongoing indoor gathering or multiple family members using personal devices in the evening. This confirms the home is actively occupied beyond the baseline and aligns with normal evening household activity.

The connected device count jumped from 11 to 16 between 17:34 and 17:35, strongly suggesting multiple guests or additional family members have arrived. This aligns with the late afternoon timeframe and is worth a quick confirmation to ensure it's expected.

The sharp device drop at 08:30 followed by a stable 11-13 count and zero camera motion confirms the children have left for school, leaving the adults home quietly indoors. This coherent pattern aligns with a normal Tuesday morning routine.

The stable presence count of 10-14 devices over the last hour, paired with zero camera activity, confirms the household is comfortably indoors and settled. This pattern aligns with typical multi-device family usage and indicates a normal, relaxed Tuesday morning routine.

The sharp drop in connected mobile devices from 17 to 11 between 08:28 and 08:34 aligns with the typical Tuesday morning school departure window, indicating Miller and Penelope have likely left the house.

Roxanne's Eight Sleep report shows an impossible 25.14h duration and an unusually high HRV (1344ms), indicating a sensor sync or calibration glitch rather than a real physiological event; a quick device check is recommended.

The sustained connection of 13–15 mobile devices on a Monday evening, paired with zero camera activity, strongly suggests a large unverified gathering (e.g., kids' sleepover or guests) where cameras are disabled, in blind spots, or motion detection is inactive. Verifying camera status and guest presence is recommended to rule out security gaps or network anomalies.

The sustained presence of 12–17 mobile devices on a typical Monday afternoon, combined with zero camera activity, strongly suggests an unannounced gathering or guest network activation, as this count far exceeds the four household members. Verification is recommended to confirm visitor status and rule out unauthorized access.

The sustained presence of 14–16 mobile devices on a typical Monday afternoon, combined with zero camera activity, strongly suggests either unlogged guests are visiting or a network presence-tracking glitch; verifying guest logs or device connectivity would clarify the situation.

A persistently high count of 14-17 mobile devices on a Monday afternoon strongly contradicts typical school and work schedules, suggesting either unrecorded early dismissals, unexpected guests, or a presence-tracking anomaly that Steve should verify.
